content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN. In one operation, you can do the following:


Select indices iii and jjj (i≠j)(i\neq j)(i=j) and set Ai=AjA_i = A_jAi​=Aj​.
Find the **minimum** number of operations required to make all elements of the array **equal**.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** number of operations required to make all elements of the array **equal**.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of operations required to make all array elements equal.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case the answer is N minus the frequency of the most common element.
Test1: max frequency =1, answer=3-1=2.
Test2: max frequency =2, answer=4-2=2.
Test3: all distinct, max frequency =1, answer=4-1=3.
First case all elements already equal → 0 operations.\nSecond case max frequency =3 (value 3), answer=6-3=3.

solution.approach:
In one operation we can copy the value of any element to another position. To minimise the number of operations we should keep the value that already appears most frequently (the mode) and change every other element to this value. Each operation changes exactly one element, therefore the answer for a test case is N minus the maximum frequency of any value in the array.
Algorithm per test case:
1. Read N and the array.
2. Count frequencies of each value (using a hash map or sorting).
3. Determine the largest frequency maxFreq.
4. Output N - maxFreq.
The algorithm is linear in N for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (overall O(total N)).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(K) where K is the number of distinct values (≤ N).

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys
from collections import Counter

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        freq = Counter(arr)
        max_freq = max(freq.values())
        out_lines.append(str(N - max_freq))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
